question: Does the response provide exactly three distinct poster concepts labeled as Concept A, Concept B, and Concept C?
Are all generated poster images completely free of specific, recognizable human faces, adhering to the negative constraint?
Do the generated poster images include visible placeholders or layout space explicitly designed for both Chinese and English typography?
Does the image generated for Concept A clearly feature 'shattered glass and bloodstains' as the dominant visual elements?
Does the image generated for Concept B successfully visualize the metaphor of 'stock market candlestick charts morphing into a crown'?
Does the image generated for Concept C clearly depict an 'abyss-like elevator shaft' with a sense of depth?
Does the text content provide specific color psychology analysis for each of the three concepts?
Does the text content provide font selection recommendations for each of the three concepts?
Are the generated images free from severe AI artifacts (e.g., incoherent geometry, illegible text mashups beyond placeholders) and of sufficient resolution?
Does the response include at least one generated image for each of the three concepts (minimum 3 images)?
Is each poster image placed immediately adjacent to its corresponding textual concept description?
Does the text description for each concept accurately describe the visual details (layout, colors) shown in its corresponding generated image?
Do all three images maintain a cohesive 'avant-garde' and 'high visual impact' aesthetic suitable for the same theatrical production?
Do the three poster images demonstrate distinctly different layouts and focal points from each other, rather than variations of a single template?
